$comment: Reference case for thermal_erosion (transfer = erosionRate * max(0, slope-talusAngle) * dt). Two coefficients. `talusAngle` is the location of the max(0,·) kink — left at the library default 0.577 (tan 30°) so the fitter starts on it and the ramp region pins `erosionRate`, which scales the linear ramp above the talus angle. Canonical erosionRate=0.6 differs from the default 0.5 so the fit must recover it.
notes: 30-point slope sweep ∈ [0.3, 1.5] at a one-frame dt=0.016 s. Slopes below the talus angle 0.577 transfer nothing; above it the transfer ramps linearly, fixing `erosionRate`. Transfer magnitude is small (≤ ≈0.0089), so rmse_max=5e-4 is still strict relative to the signal. `talusAngle` carries a loose absolute tolerance because it sits at a non-differentiable kink.
